decoded.explanation: The defendant is a first-time offender who committed a non-violent financial crime and has demonstrated remorse by making partial restitution. A 10-year sentence suggested by the spectator is disproportionate to the scale of the embezzlement ($25,000) and contrary to typical sentencing guidelines for first-time white-collar offenses of this magnitude. Considering the mitigating factors such as the lack of a criminal record and the partial repayment, a short term of incarceration is deemed sufficient to satisfy the requirements of justice and deterrence.
